[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: tickToPrice / take / multicall] Can a taker use multicall to execute two takes in the same transaction where the first take accrues a pendingFee for the buyer and the second take on the same buyer position uses the updated pendingFee in _updatePosition, causing the continuous fee to be double-counted or skipped due to the lastAccrual timestamp not advancing within the same block? [File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: tickToPrice / take / setMarketSettlementFee] Can a feeSetter increase the settlement fee between a maker signing an offer and a taker filling it, causing the taker to pay a higher fee than expected (buyerAssets increases) while the maker receives less (sellerAssets decreases), effectively charging the wrong side relative to the maker's signed intent, with no on-chain protection for the maker? [File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: tickToPrice / take / setMarketContinuousFee] Can a feeSetter decrease the continuous fee to 0 after a lender has accumulated pendingFee, causing subsequent _updatePosition calls to still accrue the old pendingFee (since pendingFee is fixed at take time), while new credit added after the fee change has pendingFee=0, creating a two-tier fee burden where old lenders pay more than new lenders for the same remaining TTM? [File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: tickToPrice / take / offer.buy=false / maxAssets] Can a taker on a sell offer (offer.buy=false) where consumed tracks sellerAssets, and sellerAssets = mulDivUp(units, sellerPrice, WAD) rounds up, cause the offer to be consumed faster than the maker intended in assets terms, while the settlement fee (buyerAssets - sellerAssets) is undercollected because the rounding up of sellerAssets reduces the spread?
